zotero.abstractNote: We present DeepGaze III, a deep learning model for predicting human scanpaths in free-viewing tasks. DeepGaze III builds on the DeepGaze IIE saliency model and extends it to predict sequences of fixations. The model uses a recurrent neural network to model the dependency of the next fixation on the previous fixations and the image content. We show that DeepGaze III outperforms existing models on the MIT/Tuebingen saliency benchmark and the SALICON dataset. Furthermore, we demonstrate that the model can be used to generate realistic scanpaths that are indistinguishable from human scanpaths in a visual Turing test.
